en: For a while, I used a Ziploc bag.
I liked the zipper. But, it didn't fit so well in my pocket.
ko: 지퍼가 좋았지만 주머니에 잘 안 들어갔고, 할머니 말씀대로,

en: And, as Nana would say, it wasn't very gentleman-like.
Then I treated myself to a fancy new wallet.
ko: 그래서 멋진 새 지갑을 하나 장만했어요.

en: Just like the plastic bag, it too had a zipper.
So, last week, I run out of my apartment, and like I do every day,
ko: 그래서 지난주에 아파트에서 뛰어나와서, 매일 하듯이 자전거를 4층 계단으로

en: I carry my bike down four flights of steps and out the door.
I was in a rush to meet Candice for coffee, where we were supposed to discuss our upcoming RV trip.
ko: 캔디스를 만나 커피를 마시며 다가오는 여행을 논의하기로 해서 서둘렀어요.

en: So, I ordered two iced lattes, which were $10.13,
ko: 아이스 라떼 두 잔을 주문했는데 10달러 13센트였어요. 지갑을 꺼내려다 없다는 걸 발견했죠.

en: only to reach for my wallet and discover I didn't have it.
ko: 아이스 라떼 두 잔을 주문했는데 10달러 13센트였어요. 지갑을 꺼내려다 없다는 걸 발견했죠.

en: Now, Candice is pissed off because she doesn't have her purse.
ko: 캔디스도 가방이 없어서 화가 났고, 저는 다시 일하러 가야 했어요.

en: So, I have to get back on my bike, ride 10 blocks home,
